verses.text: Two angels arrive at Sodom in the evening. Lot sits at the city gate — a place of public business. He greets them with deep respect, takes them home, and prepares a feast. Even in Sodom, Lot still keeps the hospitality of his uncle.

Two angels arrive at Sodom in the evening. Lot sits at the city gate — a place of public business. He greets them with deep respect, takes them home, and prepares a feast. Even in Sodom, Lot still keeps the hospitality of his uncle.

Two angels arrive at Sodom in the evening. Lot sits at the city gate — a place of public business. He greets them with deep respect, takes them home, and prepares a feast. Even in Sodom, Lot still keeps the hospitality of his uncle.

But before bed, the men of Sodom — old and young, every quarter — surround the house and demand the guests be handed over for assault. The text doesn't soften this. The wickedness God came down to investigate is on full display.

But before bed, the men of Sodom — old and young, every quarter — surround the house and demand the guests be handed over for assault. The text doesn't soften this. The wickedness God came down to investigate is on full display.

Lot steps out and offers his own daughters in place of the guests. This is one of the most horrifying offers in Scripture. Genesis does not approve — it is showing how Sodom's sickness has already infected the only man called righteous in the city.

Lot steps out and offers his own daughters in place of the guests. This is one of the most horrifying offers in Scripture. Genesis does not approve — it is showing how Sodom's sickness has already infected the only man called righteous in the city.

Lot steps out and offers his own daughters in place of the guests. This is one of the most horrifying offers in Scripture. Genesis does not approve — it is showing how Sodom's sickness has already infected the only man called righteous in the city.
The mob turns on Lot: this man came to sojourn and now wants to judge. They press hard against him, breaking the door. An outsider with a conscience is never welcome in a city this far gone.
The angels pull Lot back inside and strike the men outside with blindness. The mob keeps groping for the door, unable to find it even now. Judgment has begun — but they cannot perceive it.
The angels pull Lot back inside and strike the men outside with blindness. The mob keeps groping for the door, unable to find it even now. Judgment has begun — but they cannot perceive it.
The angels ask Lot: who else of yours is here? Get them out. We are going to destroy this place because its cry is great before the LORD. The investigation is over. The verdict is delivered.
The angels ask Lot: who else of yours is here? Get them out. We are going to destroy this place because its cry is great before the LORD. The investigation is over. The verdict is delivered.
Lot warns his sons-in-law. They think he is joking. The men who would marry Lot's daughters cannot believe the city is in danger — they are already too at home in it.

Morning comes. The angels urge Lot to hurry. He lingers. So they take his hand, his wife's hand, his daughters' hands, and physically bring them out — the LORD being merciful to him. Sometimes deliverance is grace dragging us out.

Morning comes. The angels urge Lot to hurry. He lingers. So they take his hand, his wife's hand, his daughters' hands, and physically bring them out — the LORD being merciful to him. Sometimes deliverance is grace dragging us out.
Escape for your life. Do not look behind you. Do not stop in the plain. Flee to the mountain, or you will be consumed. The instruction is simple and absolute.

Lot bargains. Please, not the mountain, that's too far. Let me flee to that little town nearby. It is little. Spare it, and let me live there. Even now, Lot wants to compromise — anywhere but the wilderness.

Lot bargains. Please, not the mountain, that's too far. Let me flee to that little town nearby. It is little. Spare it, and let me live there. Even now, Lot wants to compromise — anywhere but the wilderness.

Lot bargains. Please, not the mountain, that's too far. Let me flee to that little town nearby. It is little. Spare it, and let me live there. Even now, Lot wants to compromise — anywhere but the wilderness.
God grants the request. The little town is renamed Zoar — meaning little. Lot enters as the sun rises. The day of destruction has dawned.
God grants the request. The little town is renamed Zoar — meaning little. Lot enters as the sun rises. The day of destruction has dawned.
God grants the request. The little town is renamed Zoar — meaning little. Lot enters as the sun rises. The day of destruction has dawned.
Then the LORD rained on Sodom and Gomorrah brimstone and fire from the LORD out of heaven. The cities, the plain, the inhabitants, every growing thing — all overthrown. The whole region is unmade.
Then the LORD rained on Sodom and Gomorrah brimstone and fire from the LORD out of heaven. The cities, the plain, the inhabitants, every growing thing — all overthrown. The whole region is unmade.
But his wife looked back from behind him, and she became a pillar of salt. One backward glance — and her body became a monument to the city she could not let go of.

Early in the morning Abraham returns to the place where he had stood before the LORD. He looks toward the plain and sees the smoke rising like a furnace. His intercession had been heard — but Sodom is gone.

Early in the morning Abraham returns to the place where he had stood before the LORD. He looks toward the plain and sees the smoke rising like a furnace. His intercession had been heard — but Sodom is gone.
And God remembered Abraham, and sent Lot out of the midst of the overthrow. The verse states it plainly: Lot was saved because of Abraham's prayers. Intercession reaches further than we know.
Lot is afraid even of Zoar. He flees to a cave in the mountain with his two daughters. The man who couldn't bear the mountain has ended up there anyway, alone.
Lot is afraid even of Zoar. He flees to a cave in the mountain with his two daughters. The man who couldn't bear the mountain has ended up there anyway, alone.

His daughters, believing no men are left, decide to have children by their father. They get him drunk on two consecutive nights and each conceives. The story is told without applause or condemnation — the reader is meant to feel the wreckage Sodom left behind.

His daughters, believing no men are left, decide to have children by their father. They get him drunk on two consecutive nights and each conceives. The story is told without applause or condemnation — the reader is meant to feel the wreckage Sodom left behind.

The daughters bear Moab and Ben-ammi, ancestors of two nations that will trouble Israel for centuries. But also — Ruth, the great-grandmother of David, is a Moabite. Even from this disaster, God will weave grace.
